risks: No managed identity resource configured despite 'Secure by default with managed identity' being an architecture principle; disableLocalAuth is false on the OpenAI account, leaving key-based auth enabled
publicNetworkAccess is Enabled on both Azure OpenAI and Cosmos DB with no private endpoints or VNet integration planned
Cosmos DB serverless is limited to a single Azure region with no failover capability — a regional outage causes full data-tier unavailability
No monitoring resources (Application Insights, Log Analytics, or diagnostic settings) included for operational visibility
API Management Consumption tier has cold-start latency on first request, which may degrade the real-time Q&A experience under bursty traffic
App Service B1 (Basic) tier does not support autoscale or deployment slots, limiting production resilience and zero-downtime deployments
No Azure Key Vault resource for centralized secrets management
correctionsRecommended: Add a system-assigned managed identity to the App Service and configure Azure RBAC (Cognitive Services OpenAI User, Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor) for passwordless access; set disableLocalAuth to true on the OpenAI resource
Add an Azure Key Vault resource to store any application secrets and configure access via managed identity
Add an Application Insights resource connected to a Log Analytics workspace for end-to-end monitoring and diagnostics
Consider upgrading App Service Plan from B1 to S1+ for deployment slots or P1v3 for autoscale and zone redundancy
Evaluate adding private endpoints for Azure OpenAI and Cosmos DB to restrict data-plane access to the VNet
Consider upgrading API Management from Consumption to BasicV2 or StandardV2 to eliminate cold-start latency and enable VNet integration
Add diagnostic settings on all resources to route logs and metrics to Log Analytics
